insights.text: You can choose to lie to yourself, cheat fear, and steal back the confidence you were born with, by practicing positive affirmations.
insights.quote: I think you should lie to yourself I think you should lie to yourself I think you should liecheat and steal every day of your life lie to your mind cheat fear and steal back the confidence youwere born with
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The No.1 Celebrity Therapist: The WEIRD Trick To Get Your Sex Life Back, Fix Erections & Increase Fertility! - Marisa Peer

insights.text: Maintaining an unwavering self-belief is vital, even when facing adversity, and focusing on scoring consistently can keep the doubts at bay.
insights.quote: Every time I step on a pitchI'm thinking in my headI'm going to score todayI'm going to scoreI know I'm going to scorewhen the chance comesI'm going to score because in a game you you bounce get a chance that you should be scoring.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Ivan Toney: I’m Finally Ready To Speak! The Truth About His Football Ban And His Future Team!

insights.text: Confidence and self-belief are vital for achieving goals; back yourself no matter who you are facing.
insights.quote: Confidence in yourself, self-belief. I think the one thing I have is a lot of self-belief i'm firmly i remember a friend of mine kevin peterson the cricketer his big mantra with himself when he played cricket for england was back yourself back yourself whoever you're facing.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Piers Morgan: Dealing With Repeat Failure, Death Threats & Regrets

insights.text: You must want something better before change is possible and start acting like the person who has it.
insights.quote: You've got to start there. You've got to start with wanting something better for yourself. And then I personally think the most important thing is to start acting like the person who has the things that you want right now
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Mel Robbins: "Saying These 2 Words Will Fix Your Anxiety!" The New Trick For Stress, Anxiety & Breaking Every Bad Habit In 2024!

insights.text: Self-belief and resilience are key; even when facing setbacks, maintaining a positive attitude is essential.
insights.quote: I think that's one of the things I always try and push,is that self-belief, keep on going.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E39: Jamal Edwards - From Media Student To Multimillionaire

insights.text: True self-belief eliminates the need to seek validation from others.
insights.quote: It's never been about other people's perceptions of you it's always been what you think about yourself and if you truly truly believe in yourself you won't want to be anybody else you want to you won't want to be fake you wouldn't want to be trying to please everyone my job isn't to please everyone.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - How She Built Her Confidence, and Then an Empire with Krissy Cela

insights.text: Facing fears consistently rewrites your self-story, building confidence and mastery in unexpected areas.
insights.quote: Every fear that I have, if I master it and I go all in on it, something magical and beautiful happens on the other side.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Single Biggest Killer Of Relationships: Lewis Howes

insights.text: The five causes of suffering are: not knowing who we are, not knowing the nature of reality, clinging to ephemeral experiences, confusing ourselves with our ego identity, and fearing death.
insights.quote: Not knowing who we are, number one. Not knowing the nature of reality. No isolated self. Clinging and grasping at experience, which is ephemeral... We confuse ourselves with our ego identity, which is socially induced, and we fear death. Those are the five causes of suffering.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Stress Is Killing You! 5 Proven Steps To Reverse Its Effects: Deepak Chopra

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Consistent small steps, pursued over a long time horizon, lead to compounding results.
insights.quote: The goal isn't to have the perfect daily routine tomorrow. The goal is to still be winning your daily routine in 50 years time.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 127: How To Build Unbreakable Confidence By Making This ONE Promise To Yourself: Chris Williamson

insights.text: Goals are good for setting direction, but systems, or daily habits, are the process that ensure continuous progress.
insights.quote: Goals are best for people who care about winning once. Systems are best for people who care about winning repeatedly.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Discipline Expert: The Habit That Will Make Or Break Your Entire 2026! James Clear

insights.text: Small, consistent decisions compound over time to produce significant results, emphasizing the power of daily habits.
insights.quote: It's not those key critical big decisions we make to drop out it's that like yeah it's the the compounding small almost uh irrelevant decisions
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 140: The 2 Minute Trick That Beats Procrastinating For Good: Ali Abdaal

insights.text: Consistency in small daily routines, like breath work, reinforces self-trust and sets the tone for the day.
insights.quote: I make little promises to myself and I try to keep them. And I find that I lose confidence in myself when I consistently break really small promises to myself.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 181: 5 Things Your Body Is Probably Missing, That's Making You Sick! Gary Brecka

insights.text: The process for changing ingrained habits involves raising awareness, focused attention on triggers and consequences, and deliberate practice of new behaviors, but also requires accountability.
insights.quote: Step one is spot the pattern... next stage is deliberate practice...but the fourth factor is accountability because most people left to their own devices will give up on that process when it feels too hard.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - No.1 Neuroscientist: Why You Should Always Look Into Someone’s Left Eye! & How Stress Leaks Through Skin, Is Contagious & Gives You Belly Fat! Dr. Tara Swart

insights.text: It is crucial to turn up the internal voice and dial down the external voices of society.
insights.quote: The most important thing you can do if you want to reach your potential...is to like turn up the internal dial and just try and get that the other dial which is society's voice right down to fucking zero.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 15 - Ant Middleton: The Power Of A Positive Perspective

insights.text: Morning routines are valuable as they reduce the exposure to micro stress doses, building resilience for the day.
insights.quote: Why I think morning routines can be so valuable is they can reduce how many micro stress doses you're exposed to first thing in the morning so you are going intoyour day with much more headroom and much more resilience
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 72 - Why You Need to Care About Your Morning Routine: Dr Rangan Chatterjee

insights.text: People who successfully maintain weight loss often develop a new identity, reverse-engineering habits and behaviors from their ideal self.
insights.quote: Think of the person that you want to become. Picture them. Think about what do you think their habits and behaviors look like on a daily basis? Now start to reverse engineer that.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Calories Expert: "Health Experts Are Wrong About Calories!" "The Surprising Benefits Of Diet Coke!" & "The Link Between Obesity & Past Abuse!"

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley

insights.text: Asking a partner directly how they like to give and receive love, can significantly improve intimacy and understanding in the relationship.
insights.quote: It's been a game changer to just ask the person that I'm dating or my current partner to be like how do you like to be loved how do you like to receive love and how do you like to give it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 79 - The Surprising Truth About Sex: Africa Brooke

insights.text: Vulnerability, especially for men, can be perceived as weakness due to societal expectations, but it's crucial for building deeper connections and attracting genuine relationships.
insights.quote: And finally,thank you for teaching me how to lovein a way that I was too scared to before.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Matthew Hussey: The Secret To Building A Perfect Relationship

insights.text: Attachment and authenticity are fundamental human needs, and conflict between these can lead to trauma, where individuals sacrifice authenticity to maintain relationships.
insights.quote: The worst aspect of trauma is the disconnection from ourselves. And we do that for the sake of maintaining attachments.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Doctor Gabor Mate: I Regret My Interview With Prince Harry! The Shocking Link Between Kindness & Illness!

insights.text: The level of satisfaction in a relationship is key to overall wellbeing and health, and should be prioritized over longevity.
insights.quote: the satisfaction in the relationship isthe key...we place too muchvalue on longevity...The question should be, how satisfied areyou in your marriage or in your partnership?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Paul Brunson: Women Need To Lower Their Standards! If They Have These 3 Traits, Never Let Them Go! If You Get Sick, There’s A 624% Chance He’ll Leave!

insights.text: True love involves allowing your partner to be themselves, recognizing them as a unique individual separate from your own needs.
insights.quote: And I think if relationships are going to have any longevity at some point that has toshift into actually this person is a mystery and i might spend the rest of my life trying to get toknow this person but that's i think that's okay
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Derren Brown: UNLOCK The Secret Power Of Your Mind!

insights.text: Vulnerability fosters connection, and striving for a 'perfect' image prevents genuine relationship building.
insights.quote: But the truth is when you have a shiny, perfect exterior,there's no cracks for me to grab onto. You actually need to show me the cracks so that there's something to grip and I can actually feel connected to you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Love Expert: Why Women Are Addicted To F**k Boys & Why You Should "Have A 'Boring' Relationship Instead!" Logan Ury

insights.text: Character qualities like flexibility and emotional generosity are more critical for long-term relationship success than superficial traits.
insights.quote: If you look at what are the most important things that would predict whether a relationship is going to last, what qualities do you want in a partner? Flexibility is really important...Emotional generosity is really important that you give someone the benefit of the doubt.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Relationship Therapist: "This Statistically Is The Best Age To Get Married So You Don't Get A Divorce!", "Men Should Not Split The Bill", "80% Of Women Want Men Over 6ft When Only 15% Are 6ft!"

insights.text: It's vital to prioritize relationships with loved ones, realizing everything one is striving for is already present in those connections.
insights.quote: Everything you're working to achieve, with that perspective, you already have it.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Scooter Braun: My Divorce Saved My Life, But I Didn’t See It Coming! I Still Carry Guilt For The Young Artists I Managed!

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Financial independence is largely a mindset focused on managing expectations, not just accumulating wealth.
insights.quote: The key is managing your expectations more than it is, how can I just pile up as much money as I possibly can?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Savings Expert: They're Lying To You About Buying A House! Tariffs Are About To Skyrocket Cost Of Living! Here's The Truth About America Collapsing!

insights.text: Finance is made artificially complex to create a moat around the industry, but understanding basic balance sheets and P&L statements is key to business acquisition.
insights.quote: We just make it sound complex in finance because financial lingo is like the moat around how we make money, right? The less people speak our language, the more we can charge them, which is the secret.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 137: Finance Isn’t Rocket Science! The 2 Simple Skills Billionaires Use Everyday: Codie Sanchez

insights.text: The current financial system is rigged against the average person, as wealth creation opportunities are often reserved for VCs or institutional investors.
insights.quote: The average person watching this has no chance of making the money. They'll make normal returns, not supernormal returns.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Investing & Crypto Expert: "We Only Have 6 Years Until Everything Changes!", "The S&P 500 Isn't Worth Your Time!", "Don't Keep Spare Cash In A Bank!"

insights.text: Living below your means allows you to accumulate capital and create financial security over time.
insights.quote: The key to wealth isn't as much how much money you make. It's your ability to live a little bit like a stoic and live below your means.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Relationships Professor: “This Is An Emergency!” The Number Of Men Having No Sex Increased 180%! (Why This Is Dangerous & How We Fix It)

insights.text: Understanding the basic language of money, like savings and investment percentages, is crucial for financial literacy, much like understanding the rules of the road when driving.
insights.quote: Most of us do not have even the equivalent knowledge of money. For example, the basic language of money would be, what percentage of your income are you saving and why? What percentage are you investing and why?
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 148: Guarantee Financial Freedom! The 4 Numbers You NEED To Be Tracking: Ramit Sethi

insights.text: Having a money mindset, including believing you can become wealthy, is the critical first step to financial success.
insights.quote: You have to say, I will become wealthy. Why? Because if you don't believe you're going to become wealthy, it is going to be impossible for you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Savings Expert: Are You Under 45? You Probably Aren’t Getting A Pension! Do Not Buy A House!  This Is Probably Why You’re Broke! - Jaspreet Singh

insights.text: The primary obligation of AI companies is to maximize profits for investors, not to ensure safety or ethical behavior.
insights.quote: The only obligation they have is to make money for the investors. That's the legal obligation they have. They have no moral or ethical obligations.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Roman Yampolskiy: These Are The Only 5 Jobs That Will Remain In 2030 & Proof We're Living In a Simulation!

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Even small increases in physical activity, especially for those starting from a sedentary lifestyle, yield significant cardiovascular and brain health benefits.
insights.quote: The biggest bang for your buck from a public health standpoint are people who are starting from almost zero and just starting to exercise a little bit... getting four or 5,000 steps per day is going to give you a big cardiovascular benefit and a big benefit for your brain.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Brain Professor: "Popular Treat Now Considered Deadlier Than Smoking!", "Alcohol Is Shrinking Your Brain" - David Raichlen

insights.text: Physical activity, especially strength training, slows down senescence (bodily degradation) more than aging itself, particularly as we get older.
insights.quote: Aging is just the clock ticking on, right? There's nothing we can do about age. But senescence is the way our bodies degrade as we get older. And what physical activity does, maybe the most important thing about physical activity, is that it slows senescence.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Harvard Professor: "10,000 Steps A Day" Is A LIE! Revealing The 7 BIG LIES About Exercise, Sleep, Running, Cancer & Sugar!!! Daniel Lieberman

insights.text: Specificity in training is essential; focus on the muscles you want to develop.
insights.quote: Specificity, which is the most important principle in all of exercise science. It's telling yourself, okay, I want bigger biceps, and then focus on that.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Muscle Building Expert: “Creatine Loading Is a Waste Of Time!”, They’re Lying To You About Workout Hours! Dr Michael Israetel

insights.text: Focusing on building and maintaining muscle mass is more effective for long-term weight loss and metabolic health than relying solely on cardio.
insights.quote: When you focus on building and maintaining muscle it's going to better support your health your metabolic health um then if we're just trying to do uh the cardio exercise
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - No.1 Heart Surgeon: Cardio Is A Waste Of Time For Weight Loss! Philip Ovadia

insights.text: VO2 max is a strong predictor of all-cause mortality, highlighting the profound benefits of exercise and cardiorespiratory fitness.
insights.quote: If you compare the fittest two and a half percent to the least fit 25%, it's five, which means 400% difference in all-cause mortality.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The LIFE-EXTENSION Doctor: "The ONE thing that's increasing your chance of early-death by 170.8%!" Peter Attia (E267)

insights.text: Prioritizing muscle health through strength training and protein intake is crucial for metabolic optimization and overall well-being, especially during interventions like GLP-1 agonist therapy.
insights.quote: They need to be strength training and they need to be prioritizing their protein macros. Like those are the two non-negotiables.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Ozempic Expert: Ozempic Transforms Your Gut Microbiome! People Are Being Overdosed On Ozempic! Microdosing Saved My Life! - Dr Tyna Moore

insights.text: Women doing fasted training may burn lean mass because their hypothalamus is more sensitive to nutrient density compared to men.
insights.quote: All you've effectively done is burn through your lean mass...It would be better for you as a woman to have maybe 15 grams of protein...before you go do cardio and strength.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Exercise & Nutrition Scientist: The Truth About Exercising On Your Period! Women Were Right About Menopause! These 4 Supplements Give Women Optimal Health!

insights.text: It's more important to focus on physical readiness and performance, like strength and skill acquisition, rather than just appearance.
insights.quote: You should focus on physical readiness, but it shouldn't be about how you look...It should be about how you perform.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Anti-Obesity Doctor: If You Don't Exercise, This Is What's Happening To You! Use This Anti-Aging Cure No One Is Talking About! - Gabrielle Lyon

guests: Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: As a CEO, it is important to hire people better than you in their respective fields. By relinquishing total control, a leader empowers their team and unlocks the company's full potential.
insights.quote: You should hire people that are better than you at their specific disciplines. So like, even if you're like, if you're micromanaging, if you're being like a bottleneck, you are automatically causing a problem.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Grace Beverley: How To Build A Multi-Million Pound Empire At 24

insights.text: Having a strong, talented senior team is crucial for scaling a business; delays in hiring can lead to CEO burnout.
insights.quote: But that was the biggest problem for me that we didn't have the right senior leadership in place. So I was working several jobs.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Monzo CEO On Death Threats, Depression & Digital Banking Wars - Tom Blomfield

insights.text: Naivety in early shareholder agreements can lead to loss of control; independent legal advice is crucial to protect the founder's decision-making power.
insights.quote: I was young and naive at 20, signing a shareholders agreement, which pretty much meant that I couldn't make the decisions on behalf of the company.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E19: Luke Massie - I Have Nothing To Hide

insights.text: Micro-compliance, a series of small agreements, is a powerful technique for influencing behavior, similar to methods used in brainwashing and cult recruitment.
insights.quote: This is how social media starts roping you in. This is how politics starts roping you in. This is how cult leaders will recruit you into a cult. It's the number one way that we influence another human being, micro-compliance.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Manipulation Expert: How To Influence Anyone & Make Them Do Exactly What You Want! - Chase Hughes

insights.text: It's important to connect with others and seek external perspectives, as the voice in your head can be biased and lead to poor decisions.
insights.quote: I don't believe anyone should listen to voices in their head if I'm honest with you...You should always have someone to bounce something off.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Ashley Walters (Top Boy): "Me & Kano Didn't Have The Greatest Time!". The Unheard TRUTH About Top Boy, Career Rejection & Personal Grief!

insights.text: It's a leader's responsibility to communicate effectively and bridge the gap between their vision and their team's understanding, requiring patience and clear articulation.
insights.quote: Trying to communicate that to other people who, for whatever reason, might not be as strong in that field or it might not make as much sense to them, that barrier can sometimes be a little frustrating.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E28: Tobi Pearce - £100m in Revenue Aged 27

insights.text: Transparency as a leader encourages honesty from your team, leading to better data for decision-making.
insights.quote: The way to get transparency from your team is first, first you've got to give it to them.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Uber CEO: At Uber, If You Don’t Perform, You’re Out! Uber Was Losing $3b A Year

insights.text: A key leadership principle is to shift the focus from controlling individuals to empowering them to make decisions related to their own performance.
insights.quote: We're going to take the crown off the heads of the coaches and put them onto the heads of the riders and they're going to be the come the kings and queens of their own world their own destiny and we're going to support them in that.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The “Winning Expert”: How To Become The Best You Can Be: Sir David Brailsford

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley
Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Prioritize delivering value over simply working hard; smart work is more effective than hard work.
insights.quote: Really what you should focus on is delivering more value because someone in the director level can deliver the same amount of value in 10 minutes that an intern could potentially deliver in a day.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E7: Ben Francis - 25 year old CEO OF UK’s fastest growing company

insights.text: It's important to disconnect from arbitrary time-based achievements and focus on the process of working towards your goals, trusting that things will work out eventually.
insights.quote: I disconnected myself from any arbitrary time-based achievement, which freaks people out, especially if you're on a quarterly or annual financial schedule.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 118 - The Ultimate Gift YOU Should Be Offering Each & Every Friend: Simon Sinek

insights.text: Instead of focusing solely on outputs (goals), plan for the inputs (the time and activities required) to achieve those outputs.
insights.quote: But as knowledge workers, we only think about the output... So instead of saying, get organized, I want you instead to say, organize bag for one hour. No output, only input.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E43: Nir Eyal - How to Become Indistractable

insights.text: Overcoming the fear of rejection is a superpower that enables you to pursue your goals with singular focus.
insights.quote: If you can get over that, it is a superpower in life...Because I stopped thinking about it not working.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Oz Pearlman (Mentalist): This Small Mistake Makes People Dislike You! They Do This, They’re Lying!

insights.text: Circadian disruption, even from being awake a few hours late a couple of times a week, significantly elevates the risk of various diseases.
insights.quote: If you're awake for two hours between 10 p.m. and 4 a.m. for two days per week for 25 days of the year, you qualify as a kind of card carrying shift worker and you likely have significant circadian disruption.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Fitness Scientist: "Even A Little Alcohol Is Hurting Your Health!", "Late Night Screen Time Linked To Cancer!", "Working Shifts Kills You 15 Years Early!" - Kristen Holmes

insights.text: Hard work alone doesn't guarantee success; strategic focus is more important.
insights.quote: If you're smart about the things that you choose to focus on, you make 99% of the difference.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 193: Hard Work Doesn't Equal Success…Try This Instead...: Former Netflix CEO

insights.text: Goals related to faith, family, friendship, and serving others lead to more fulfilling lives than those focused on money, power, pleasure, or fame.
insights.quote: The four goals that really matter are faith, family, friendship, and work that serves others. Those are the four really great and transcendent goals that we can have.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Most Replayed Moment: Why You’re Never Satisfied! The 4 Pillars of Lasting Happiness

insights.text: Emotions are not to be judged but seen as information; they are your brain's best guess at what's happening, and it's your job to work out if it's right or wrong.
insights.quote: Emotions get such a bad rap don't they...actually the emotion isn't the thing to judge the emotionis information it's your brain's best guess at what might be going on around youand your brain sometimes gets it right and sometimes gets it wrong and it's your job towork that out.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - World Leading Psychologist: How To Detach From Overthinking & Anxiety: Dr Julie Smith

guests: Benjamin Weinthal, Annie Jacobsen, Andrew Bustamante

insights.text: Curiosity is essential for innovation, requiring inspiration from outside one's own sector.
insights.quote: Find inspiration outside of your sector. And that's that idea of bringing the outside in.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 77 - Nike CMO: Three Steps to Genius Marketing: Greg Hoffman

insights.text: The worst part about pursuing creative dreams in Hollywood, or similar highly competitive fields, is the uncertainty and constant possibility of rejection.
insights.quote: Any given phone call is one that is like making your fucking life or one that is yet another door slamming in your face.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Seth Rogen Opens Up About His Self-Doubt & Struggles That Nobody Sees!

insights.text: True artistic freedom lies in articulating the frequency of one's soul, not just creating what others will like.
insights.quote: I look at creativity as um articulate in your soul and that that's the true form for me...you're transmitting and you're articulating the sound or the frequency of your soul to a person
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Labrinth: The Musical Genius Behind Euphoria

insights.text: Trusting your instincts and being authentic are key to creativity and innovation.
insights.quote: Yeah, I think that's the key, right? To everything, in my opinion, is authenticity. And in order to be authentic, you have to trust yourself.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E200: Annie Macmanus: The Real Reason I Became Europe's No. 1 Female DJ

insights.text: Early on, businesses should focus on proven models and not try to innovate too soon, to avoid unnecessary experimentation and ensure initial success.
insights.quote: So if you do twist in the beginning, it's kind of like you're kind of thinking of an analogy. Like if you're on a long road trip and you start taking detours early on...he's going to end up hundreds of miles away from his destination.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Passive Income Expert: How To Make 10k Per Month In 90 Days!

insights.text: Failure is a critical part of the creative process, and the fear of others' opinions is a major obstacle.
insights.quote: I think failure is like the greatest gift...And I think the fear of failure is that it's a fear of what other people are going to think about you.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Casey Neistat (The Untold Story): "I was a homeless dad at 15 & had $200k debt!"...The Crazy Story Of How I Became The World's No.1 Video Creator!

guests: Alex Hormozi, Cody Sanchez, Daniel Priestley

insights.text: Eating food in a specific order (veggies, protein/fats, then starches/sugars) can reduce glucose spikes by up to 75% without changing the meal.
insights.quote: If you eat the ingredients in the meal in a specific order, you can reduce the glucose spike of that meal by up to 75% without changing how much you're eating, what you're eating.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Scary New Research On Sugar & How They Made You Addicted To It! Jessie Inchauspé

insights.text: The source of calories matters more than the total number; ultra-processed foods can lead to overeating due to their effects on gut microbes and sugar spikes.
insights.quote: The group with the ultra-processed foods overate by about 200 calories every day. They kept coming back to the buffet for more.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 107 - Why Counting Calories Doesn't Work: Tim Spector

insights.text: The best diet for long-term weight loss is one with enough protein and total calories, comprised of healthy food choices that fit individual preferences.
insights.quote: The diet with enough protein, enough total calories, that is comprised predominantly of healthy food choices, that fits the individual's personal preferences and tolerances.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Fat Burning Expert: The Real Reason You Can’t Lose Weight! PCOS, Menopause & Stubborn Belly Fat

insights.text: Products labeled with 'health halos' like 'low fat' or 'high protein' are often highly processed and can be detrimental to gut health.
insights.quote: So the combination of what we call health halos, saying it's super healthy with richness, vitamin, no sugar, no fat, is very old-fashioned science.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Food Doctor: "Extra Protein Is Making You Fatter!" The 6 Food Lies Everyone Still Believes! (Brand NEW Food Science) - Tim Spector

insights.text: Time-restricted eating is more about nutrient deficiency and glucose drop to trigger healing mechanisms than simply calorie restriction.
insights.quote: You're creating an internal mechanism with time restriction that is going to stimulate a healing process, and it's nutrient deficiency along with glucose drop. It doesn't have to do with calories.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Fasting Expert: "The Truth About Ozempic", These 3 Foods Are Leading To Cancer! Our Supermarket Food Isn't Safe! - Dr Mindy Pelz

insights.text: Foods labeled as 'low calorie' or 'low fat' often contain artificial sweeteners or replacements that can be detrimental to health.
insights.quote: Anything that says low calorie that says means they've had to add in lots of artificial sweeteners or protein extracts or something else is also a big danger sign low in fat means they've replaced the natural fat with something else that's cheaper.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 178: They're Lying To About Low Fat Foods, THIS Is What They're Doing!: Tim Spector

insights.text: Slowing down your eating rate by 20% can lead to a reduction in calorie intake of approximately 15% due to hormonal responses.
insights.quote: On average, if you change the speed in which you eat your food by about 20%, you reduce your calorie intake by about 15%.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Lead Nutritional Scientist: Seed Oils Are Not Bad For You! Eating This Twice A Day Will Help Menopause! The Alarming Link Between Chewing & Belly Fat!

insights.text: The body possesses five key health defenses: blood vessels (angiogenesis), immunity, and three others, all crucial for longevity.
insights.quote: Besides the foods you eat, other things that can compromise your health defenses, and by the way, there are five health defenses...You want to, if you want longevity, you need all five of your health defenses and more to be working in your favor.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Chronic Disease Expert: We Can Now Reverse Stage 4 Cancer! This Common Food Is Feeding Your Cancer Cells!

insights.text: Consistent sleep patterns, particularly bedtime and wake-up times, are crucial for sleep quality and are as important as sleep duration.
insights.quote: A lot of data will suggest that sleep consistency is more important than total sleep time. Meaning you need to be going to bed and waking up at roughly the same time.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Muscle Growth Doctor: "The Anti-Ageing Cure No One Is Talking About!", "Exercising At Night Is A Terrible Idea!", "Your Grip Strength Predicts Chronic Diseases!" - Andy Galpin

insights.text: Sleep is crucial for brain detoxification through the glymphatic system, which actively flushes out toxins associated with neurodegenerative diseases.
insights.quote: Those things are being flushed out of the brain very actively overnight and that process takes seven to eight hours to complete the cleaning that's why you need to be in bed for eight to nine hours
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 156: Your Stress Is Making you Fat & Sick... Here's How To Fix It!

insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Chronic Disease Expert: We Can Now Reverse Stage 4 Cancer! This Common Food Is Feeding Your Cancer Cells!

insights.text: Sports science research has historically been based on male data, generalizing findings to women which can be detrimental.
insights.quote: Everything from training to eating to recovery is based on male data. And women have been generalized to that data.
insights.episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Exercise & Nutrition Scientist: The Truth About Exercising On Your Period! Women Were Right About Menopause! These 4 Supplements Give Women Optimal Health!
